translated: It was a time when Shizuku felt she wasn't suited to be the center of Cheerful Days and was feeling really down,
but by meeting those juniors, she realized she was carrying their futures on her shoulders too.
She felt that if she lacked confidence, she had to polish herself until she found it.
This...
this was another important encounter for her, meeting those juniors.
That was a scene that really moved me and that I personally loved.
Also, the Momojan members... After Shizuku passed the audition this time,
while she was practicing silently by herself,
they brought her some snacks and things. In the card stories,
that sense of distance they have at that moment
is depicted really well.
Airi-chan especially knows Shizuku from her Cheerful Days era so well,
so she can feel Shizuku's level of commitment without needing any words. That's why Airi-chan intentionally didn't say anything,
and just watched over her. That dynamic within Momojan...
it really makes me feel all over again what wonderful girls they are.
Shizuku was like a string pulled taut the whole time,
with this intense tension and a sense of fragility, but Airi-chan was happy to see Shizuku looking like she did back when she was the center of Cheerful Days.
It's just so emotional, really.
But Airi-chan also knows how much Shizuku suffered back then.
Of course, she doesn't say that to Shizuku directly,
but she takes all of that into account while watching her grow. That's their relationship.
I really felt like saying "Thank you for writing this"
during the recording session.
Looking at the comments... Yes, everyone, it's all about trust.
Exactly.
Now, about Shizuku's side story, part two.
It's a nice way of giving back.
Everyone was thinking about what they could do for Shizuku and taking action.
Since she was being stoic and restricting her diet, they brought her snacks
that were high in nutrition and low in fat. That's just pure love, honestly.
